name: tasklist-tool
description: A VS Code extension for structured task and artifact management for AI agents.

languageModelTools.name: list_tasks
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Retrieve a list of tasks from the workspace. Use this when you need to see available tasks, check their status, or find a specific task ID to work on. You can optionally filter by status ('open', 'in-progress', 'closed'). Returns an array of task objects containing id, status, createdAt, and updatedAt.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Lists tasks in the current workspace, optionally filtered by status.

status.description: Optional status to filter tasks by.

languageModelTools.name: create_task
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Create a new task in the workspace. Use this when you are starting a new piece of work that requires tracking. The taskId must be a unique, URL-friendly string. Initial status will be 'open'. This tool creates the task entry in the index and the corresponding directory for artifacts.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Creates a new task with a unique ID.

taskId.description: A unique identifier for the task (e.g., 'feature-login-implementation').

languageModelTools.name: activate_task
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Set a specific task as the 'active' task for the current workspace session. Use this so that subsequent artifact operations default to this task without needing to provide the taskId every time. Only one task can be active at a time.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Sets a task as the active task for the workspace.

taskId.description: The ID of the task to activate.

languageModelTools.name: deactivate_task
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Clear the currently active task. Use this when you are finished working on the active task but haven't necessarily closed it yet, or when switching context away from task management.

languageModelTools.name: start_task
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Transition a task from 'open' to 'in-progress'. Use this when you actually begin working on a task. A task must be in 'open' status to be started. This is a primary state transition that signals active development.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Moves a task from 'open' to 'in-progress' status.

taskId.description: The ID of the task to start.

languageModelTools.name: close_task
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Transition a task from 'in-progress' to 'closed'. Use this when all work and documentation (artifacts) for the task are complete. A task must be in 'in-progress' status to be closed. This is the final state in the task lifecycle.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Moves a task from 'in-progress' to 'closed' status.

taskId.description: The ID of the task to close.

languageModelTools.name: list_artifact_types
languageModelTools.modelDescription: List all registered artifact types and their associated templates. Use this to discover what kind of documentation you can create (e.g., 'implementation-plan', 'walkthrough'). Returns type IDs, display names, and descriptions.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Lists all available artifact types and templates.

languageModelTools.name: list_artifacts
languageModelTools.modelDescription: List artifact files associated with a specific task. If taskId is not provided, it defaults to the currently active task. Use this to see what documentation has already been created for a piece of work. Returns only artifacts that exist on disk.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Lists artifacts for a specific task (defaults to active).

taskId.description: The ID of the task. Defaults to the active task if omitted.

languageModelTools.name: get_artifact
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Retrieve the content of a specific artifact for a task. If the artifact file already exists, it returns the current content. If it doesn't exist, it returns the default template for that artifact type so you can fill it in. Defaults to active task if taskId is omitted.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Reads an artifact's content or returns its template if it doesn't exist.

artifactType.description: The ID of the artifact type (e.g., 'research').

taskId.description: The ID of the task. Defaults to the active task if omitted.

languageModelTools.name: update_artifact
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Create or overwrite an artifact file with new content. Use this to save your research, plans, or walkthroughs. The content should be the full Markdown body (no frontmatter). Defaults to active task if taskId is omitted.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Creates or updates an artifact file with provided content.

artifactType.description: The ID of the artifact type.

content.description: The full Markdown content for the artifact.

taskId.description: The ID of the task. Defaults to the active task if omitted.

languageModelTools.name: register_artifact_type
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Register a new custom artifact type and template at the workspace level. Use this when you need a standardized document structure that isn't provided by default. Custom types are saved to '.tasks/templates/' and are available immediately.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Registers a new custom artifact type and template.

id.description: A unique ID for the custom artifact type.

displayName.description: Human-friendly name for the artifact type.

description.description: Description of what this artifact should contain.

templateBody.description: Optional Markdown template body for the new type.

scripts.pretest: npm run compile && npm run lint
